eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden kurtis show strong, clean demand across premium (Nykaa), mass (Myntra), and value (Meesho) channels with minimal discount distortion. Myntra's high rank and low discount indicate style-driven purchase intent. Meesho's high volume and accelerating reseller growth suggest expanding reach in tier-2/3/4 markets.

fusion-palazzo.upside_summary: Strong convergence across Myntra, Meesho, and internal POS indicates robust demand for fusion kurti with palazzo sets. The set format drives value perception, leading to high sell-through and repeat purchases. Internal data shows stockouts in 68 stores, suggesting unmet demand that can be captured.
fusion-palazzo.catch_summary: Nykaa's absence signals no premium demand, limiting price ceiling. Myntra's paid visibility and discount may inflate apparent demand; organic full-price demand is unproven. Meesho's lower rating (3.6) hints at quality concerns that could affect returns.

nykaa.reason: No data available; cannot assess demand at premium tier.

myntra.reason: Strong signal but paid visibility and discount introduce noise; rank weight halved.

meesho.reason: High volume, many resellers, broad regional coverage; rating is moderate but acceptable for value segment.

internal.reason: High sell-through, good margin, low returns, strong repeat rate; ground truth data.

fusion-palazzo.bet_rationale: Multiple clean signals converge: Myntra and Meesho show strong demand, internal POS confirms success with stockouts. The set format is a proven value prop. Expand to more stores and regions.
fusion-palazzo.key_uncertainty: Will next season's designs maintain the same velocity, or will design fatigue reduce demand?

convergences.title: Nykaa and Myntra converge on strong demand for Chanderi silk straight kurti
convergences.detail: Both show high ratings, low discount, and strong presence. Nykaa at premium, Myntra at mass premium.
convergences.proves: Demand exists at ₹899-1,825 with clean signals

chanderi-straight.upside_summary: Nykaa and Myntra show strong, clean demand for Chanderi silk straight kurti at ₹899-1,825 with near-zero discount. The trend has premium origin and trickle-down potential. If durability concerns are addressed (e.g., blended fabric), the style could capture metro and premium mass customers.
chanderi-straight.catch_summary: Internal POS data from a similar A-line SKU shows underperformance: 38% sell-through, 18% returns, and metro-only viability. Meesho presence is weak with an imitation product at ₹349, suggesting limited tier-2/3/4 demand. The fabric's delicacy may limit repeat purchases.

nykaa.reason: Full-price products, high ratings, editorial placement — clean premium signal

myntra.reason: Strategic Value Pricing, high rank, good ratings — clean demand signal

meesho.reason: Imitation product, low rating, weak presence — signal is weak but not noise

chanderi-straight.bet_rationale: External signals are strong but internal POS contradicts. A small batch in metro stores with a blended fabric variant can validate whether the straight cut and lower price point overcome past durability issues.
chanderi-straight.key_uncertainty: Will a straight cut at ₹699-899 with a blended fabric (e.g., Chanderi-cotton) achieve better sell-through and lower returns than the previous A-line pure silk version?

convergences.proves: The trend has broad appeal from tier-2/3 to metro markets.

convergences.proves: Our store base has proven demand for this category.

blockprint-cotton.upside_summary: Block-print cotton kurti is a proven category with strong demand across channels. Internal POS shows high sell-through and repeat buy rate. Meesho volume confirms mass appeal. Opportunity to capture demand that we under-ordered last time.
blockprint-cotton.catch_summary: Myntra/Ajio demand shows moderate discount dependency, suggesting price sensitivity. Meesho's lower price point (₹249) may undercut our pricing. Design fatigue is a risk if styles are too similar to past season.

nykaa.reason: Absent but expected; no negative signal.

blockprint-cotton.bet_rationale: Internal POS confirms strong past performance with stockouts, and external sources show broad demand. The discount dependency on Myntra/Ajio is a medium concern but does not outweigh the convergence of signals. Increase order quantity by ~20% based on past under-ordering.

reasoning_trace.signal: Similar past SKU: 52% sell-through (target 65%), 32% margin (target 40%), 22% markdown. Regional: strong in West, weak in South/East.
reasoning_trace.proves: Bandhani is a regional bet, not national. Proven demand in Gujarat/Rajasthan/Maharashtra.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether a different design/color could unlock South/East demand.
reasoning_trace.tension: Internal data contradicts national scalability implied by Myntra and Meesho. Regional pattern is a key constraint.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Meesho volume at ₹279 vs target price ₹499-799 creates price sensitivity tension. Internal POS shows regional demand, not national — contradicts any assumption of pan-India appeal from Myntra/Meesho. Nykaa weak presence suggests premium demand is not yet proven.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources show some demand for Bandhani prints. Myntra and Meesho both show positive signals (clean discount, accelerating resellers). Internal POS confirms strong demand in Western India.

bandhani-straight.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Meesho shows strong volume at ₹279, but target price band is ₹499-799; internal POS reveals regional demand, not national.

convergences.title: Bandhani print has genuine demand in Western India
convergences.detail: Meesho shows strong volume in Gujarat, Rajasthan, Maharashtra. Internal POS confirms top regions Ahmedabad, Surat, Jaipur. Myntra product (Libas) likely sells well in those regions too.
convergences.proves: Bandhani is a proven trend in Western India at multiple price points.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand outside West India.

convergences.title: Clean demand signal at mass retail (Myntra) and grassroots (Meesho)
convergences.detail: Myntra product has Strategic Value Pricing (low discount, good rating). Meesho shows accelerating reseller growth. Both indicate genuine style preference, not just discount-driven.
convergences.proves: Bandhani print has organic demand beyond deep discounting.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that demand is strong enough at ₹499-799 to justify national rollout.

bandhani-straight.upside_summary: Bandhani print has proven grassroots demand in Western India at ultra-low prices (₹279) and a clean signal at mass retail (₹649). Reseller growth on Meesho is accelerating (+35% MoM), indicating the trend is gaining momentum. If the price point can be justified at ₹499-799, there is potential to capture value-fashion demand in West India and possibly beyond.
bandhani-straight.catch_summary: The price gap between Meesho's ₹279 and the target ₹499-799 is significant — demand may be highly price-sensitive. Internal POS data shows Bandhani is a regional trend, not national, with poor performance in South and East India. Nykaa's weak premium presence suggests limited trickle-down potential. A national buy would likely repeat past mistakes.

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found, discounted, moderate reviews. Presence is emerging but weak.

meesho.reason: Strong volume, accelerating resellers, good rating. Price point is far below target band.

bandhani-straight.key_uncertainty: Will consumers in West India pay ₹599-699 for a Bandhani kurti when Meesho offers similar at ₹279?

convergences.proves: Product quality and style are well-received.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether repeat purchases will occur (internal shows low repeat rate).

organza-embroidered.upside_summary: Organza embroidered kurtis show strong demand at premium and mass price points with low discount distortion. Internal POS confirms past success with 71% sell-through and 48% margin. The trend is validated by editorial placement and high ratings.
organza-embroidered.catch_summary: Meesho presence is weak, suggesting limited tier-2/3 adoption. Internal data warns of a narrow 3-week festive window and low repeat rate. Over-committing could lead to post-festive markdowns.

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found, discount present, but editorial placement adds credibility.

myntra.reason: 2 products with clean strategic pricing, high ratings, and no noise flags.

meesho.reason: Weak signal with low rating and modest volume; data is point-in-time.

internal.reason: Ground truth from own stores with clear success metrics and actionable insight.

organza-embroidered.bet_rationale: All sources converge on festive demand with clean signals, but Meesho weakness and narrow window limit confidence. A small trial in metro stores during pre-festive period can validate timing and tier-2 potential.
organza-embroidered.key_uncertainty: Will Meesho demand accelerate as festive season approaches, or is the trend inherently premium?
watch_triggers: Meesho reseller growth accelerates above +10% MoM
Myntra rank improves to top 5 without increased discount
Nykaa full-price products appear (no discount)
Internal POS pre-festive sell-through exceeds 50% in first 2 weeks
missing_evidence: Nykaa full-price product data (all discounted)
Meesho review velocity and repeat purchase data
Competitor pricing and assortment depth
organza-embroidered.demand_integrity: Clean - Strategic Value Pricing on Myntra, low discount on Nykaa, internal success. Meesho signal is weak but not distorted.

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product at ₹1,795, 20% off, 4.2★, 150 reviews, featured in editorial edit.
reasoning_trace.proves: Premium D2C brands are testing linen straight kurti with mandarin collar at ₹1,795. Editorial placement suggests trend potential in workwear.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at mass price points (₹699-999). Single product, moderate signal, no full-price sales.
reasoning_trace.tension: Nykaa's premium positioning (₹1,795) conflicts with target price band (₹699-999). Trickle-down potential exists but unproven.

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 products at ₹799, 15% off, avg rank 28.5, avg rating 3.85, 23 reviews/30d. Discount context: 'Moderate Discount — Unclear Driver'.
reasoning_trace.proves: Organized mass retail has moderate presence at ₹799. Low discount (15%) suggests some genuine demand, but rank and reviews are modest.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand is style-driven vs price-driven. Discount context flags uncertainty. Low review velocity (down-weighted 25%) weakens signal.
reasoning_trace.tension: Myntra/Ajio signal is early/weak, conflicting with Nykaa's emerging premium signal. Both are weak but directionally aligned.

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product at ₹399, 220+ units sold, 3.2★, 85 reviews, 12 resellers, +3% MoM growth, not accelerating.
reasoning_trace.proves: Price-sensitive mass market shows weak volume at ₹399. Reseller count low, growth flat. Not a leading indicator.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at ₹699-999. Low rating (3.2) and weak reseller growth suggest limited appeal even at deep discount.
reasoning_trace.tension: Meesho's weak signal conflicts with any positive demand narrative. Suggests the style may not resonate in tier-2/3/4 markets.

reasoning_trace.signal: Similar SKU (Linen Straight Kurti SS23) stocked in 60/220 stores, 29% sell-through, 45% markdown, 24% return rate, 3% repeat. Underperformed.
reasoning_trace.proves: Linen kurtis failed for our value-fashion customer base. High returns due to wrinkling. Only worked in South Mumbai.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether a linen-blend (less wrinkling) at lower price could succeed. The past SKU was pure linen.
reasoning_trace.tension: Internal POS strongly contradicts any positive external signal. HIGH conflict: our ground truth says linen kurtis don't work for our base.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Internal POS (past failure) vs Nykaa/Myntra (emerging/moderate signals). Meesho weak adds to negative. Nykaa premium price point far above target band.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All external sources show weak-to-moderate signals. No source shows strong, clean demand. Myntra/Ajio and Nykaa both show presence but with caveats.

linen-chinese-collar.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Internal POS shows past failure with linen kurtis, while external signals are weak and discordant. No convergence.

conflicts.title: Internal POS past failure vs external emerging signals
conflicts.nykaa_says: Premium D2C testing linen straight kurti at ₹1,795 with editorial support.
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate presence at ₹799 with low discount, but demand integrity unclear.
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak volume at ₹399, low rating, flat reseller growth.
conflicts.internal_says: Similar linen kurti underperformed: 29% sell-through, 45% markdown, 24% returns. Our customer rejects linen.
conflicts.buyer_question: Can a linen-blend at ₹699-999 overcome the wrinkling/ironing issue that caused past failure?
conflicts.proves: Our customer base has rejected pure linen kurtis in the past. External signals are too weak to override this.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether a linen-blend (less wrinkling) at a lower price point could change the outcome.

conflicts.title: Nykaa premium price vs target mass price band
conflicts.nykaa_says: Product at ₹1,795, 20% off. Premium positioning.
conflicts.myntra_says: Products at ₹799, 15% off. Mass retail price.
conflicts.meesho_says: Product at ₹399. Deep value price.
conflicts.internal_says: Target price band ₹699-999. Past SKU was at similar price point.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the Nykaa signal relevant for our ₹699-999 price band, or is it a different customer?
conflicts.proves: Nykaa shows premium D2C interest, but at 2x our target price. Trickle-down is possible but unproven.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that demand at ₹1,795 translates to demand at ₹699-999.

convergences.title: All external signals are weak
convergences.detail: Nykaa (emerging, 1 product), Myntra/Ajio (moderate, low review velocity), Meesho (weak, flat growth). No source shows strong, clean demand.
convergences.proves: The trend is not yet validated across any channel. Early stage with limited evidence.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the trend will not gain traction. It may be too early.

linen-chinese-collar.upside_summary: Nykaa editorial placement and Myntra/Ajio presence at low discount suggest some initial interest in linen straight kurti with Chinese collar. If the wrinkling issue is solved with a linen-blend, there may be untapped demand in workwear segment.
linen-chinese-collar.catch_summary: Internal POS data is a strong red flag: similar pure linen kurti failed with 29% sell-through and 24% returns. Meesho shows weak traction even at ₹399. The style may not resonate with value-fashion customers who prioritize low-maintenance fabrics.

nykaa.reason: Single product, moderate signal, no full-price sales. Editorial placement adds weight but limited data.

myntra.reason: Two products, low discount, but review velocity down-weighted 25% due to unclear discount driver. Moderate presence.

meesho.reason: Weak volume, low rating, flat growth. Data is point-in-time but consistent with weak demand.

internal.reason: Ground truth from 60 stores. Clear underperformance with high returns. Highly reliable for our customer base.

linen-chinese-collar.bet_rationale: Internal POS strongly contradicts external signals. Past failure with linen kurtis is a critical red flag. External signals are too weak to justify even a small trial. Wait for evidence that a linen-blend version can overcome the wrinkling issue.
linen-chinese-collar.key_uncertainty: Can a linen-blend (less wrinkling) at ₹699-999 achieve acceptable sell-through and return rates in our stores?
watch_triggers: Nykaa adds more products or full-price sales
Myntra/Ajio rank improves above 20 with higher review velocity
Meesho reseller growth accelerates above 10% MoM
Internal POS test of linen-blend kurti shows improved sell-through
missing_evidence: Consumer survey data on linen vs linen-blend preference
Return rate data for linen-blend kurtis from other retailers
Regional demand data beyond Mumbai for linen workwear
linen-chinese-collar.demand_integrity: Low integrity: external signals are weak and discordant; internal data strongly negative. No clean convergence.
